Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

headline: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with a
body_text: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with anticipation. "I want Victoria to be my mommy."Everyone burst into laughter, their eyes on me dripping with mockery.Victoria is my husband's chief of staff. He's the CEO of a private equity group. This wasn't the first time my son had said he wanted her as his mother.I didn't want to make a scene at the Hamptons estate, so I let it slide.But when the family portrait from the party arrived at our Upper East Side penthouse, I saw it. My face had been expertly edited and replaced with Victoria's.I asked the photographer. He told me my son had demanded the change.And Richard had approved it.In that moment, I felt nothing but sheer exhaustion....That night, I told Richard I wanted a separation. He didn't even look up from his Bloomberg Terminal."Over a photo? Really?""Yes.""How many times is this now, Clara? Can you stop treating divorce like some kind of game?"Richard pinched the bridge of his nose, looking utterly weary."This time it's real, Richard. Let's contact the lawyers."I stood in front of his massive desk, my voice so steady it sounded foreign to me.Richard just scoffed dismissively and gathered up the M&A documents on the desk. "I don't have time for your theatrics. I have a conference call with the Tokyo executives at dawn."Victoria was waiting by the study door, his bespoke suit jacket draped over her arm. Her posture was rigidly perfect. "Clara, this meeting was initiated directly by several major Wall Street shareholders. It would reflect poorly on the stock price if Richard is late."She looked at me with a hint of reproach, as if I were a child throwing a tantrum.A surge of anger ignited in my chest. I snapped, "Who gave you permission to speak in this house?""Clara!" Richard slammed his hand on the desk, rattling his whiskey glass.Victoria's eyes reddened. Her voice tightened. "Richard, I'm fine. Please don't fight with her because of me."Just then, her phone rang.When she answered, she "accidentally" hit the speakerphone button. A smug male voice blasted from the receiver."Victoria, where's your star CEO? Not on the road yet?""Don't tell me that hysterical control freak has him locked down at home again?""Honestly, Richard should just sign the papers with that neurotic wife of his and marry you. Look at you—Harvard grad, young, gorgeous, sharp as a tack. You and Richard are the real 'power couple'."I recognized the voice. It was Carter, Richard's business partner.He used to lay it on thick, always calling me the perfect hostess of high society."Carter, don't be ridiculous. We'll be down in a minute."Victoria fumbled to hang up, but a faint blush crept up her ears."I'm sorry, Clara. Carter was just making one of those crass Silicon Valley jokes."I ignored her. I stared hard at Richard. "So this is how your partner talks about me behind my back? A control freak? A hysterical lunatic?"Richard rubbed his temples in frustration. "Clara, why do you always have to play the victim and nitpick every little thing?""Whatever people say is protected by the First Amendment. I can't police that."He grabbed his briefcase and moved to walk past me.As he brushed by, the scent of his cologne—identical to Victoria's—snapped the last thread of my sanity.I threw myself in front of him like a woman possessed. My voice was shrill, unrecognizable. "Call your lawyer! Right now!"Richard's face darkened. His jaw was clenched tight, and the veins on his hand gripping the briefcase bulged. "It's just a photo! Is this really the hill you want to die on?""I already told PR to track down the original files and reprint them for next month's country club newsletter. Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. He didn't even get a plea deal before the FBI hauled him away.His trust assets were frozen. Every ally in politics and business vanished overnight.Richard's parents were terrified the scandal would taint their precious family name.The day after my dad was sentenced, they summoned me to their estate in Connecticut and pressured me to sign a postnuptial agreement and file for divorce.Then I got pregnant. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. I only kept my trust beneficiary rights if I produced a male heir.When I went into labor, his mother insisted on a "natural" birth for "optimal neurological development." She paid off the high-end private doctor to refuse me an epidural.I nearly bled out on that table. I gave birth to Leo through hell and back.When I woke up, the first thing I heard was his mother's icy voice. "Stubborn as a weed... what a survivor."After that, I was diagnosed with severe postpartum depression.They used my need for psychiatric care as grounds for a protective order and took Leo back to the Boston family estate.I had to put on a cheerful mask for the therapist every single day just to earn a supervised visit on weekends.When I finally recovered and Leo came back to me, he had learned to despise me from his grandparents.He never called me Mom. It was always "that crazy woman" or "the loser." To outsiders, he said I was just an overpaid nanny.I was desperate to fix our relationship. I ignored the psychological abuse and twisted myself into knots trying to please him.But he only had eyes for Victoria.Before the birthday party, I overheard him begging Richard for a brand-new, limited-edition, professional-grade drone.I cashed in every favor I had left to get that drone. I planned to surprise him when he blew out the candles.But when the host asked him his wish, he shot me a look of pure malice and shouted into the microphone: "I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bullet tearing through my chest.The snickers and pitying glances from the society guests were unbearable. And Richard just stood there, letting it happen.I swallowed the humiliation and pain. I kept smiling, kept playing the gracious hostess, greeting guests as they came and went.And in the end, I couldn't even get a proper family photo.This time, I was just done.I wanted out of this suffocating nightmare."Richard, you're not walking out that door until your lawyer sends the email."I grabbed his shirt cuff, refusing to let go."Clara! Are you trying to drive this entire family into a mental institution? Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. He didn't even get a plea deal before the FBI hauled him away.His trust assets were frozen. Every ally in politics and business vanished overnight.Richard's parents were terrified the scandal would taint their precious family name.The day after my dad was sentenced, they summoned me to their estate in Connecticut and pressured me to sign a postnuptial agreement and file for divorce.Then I got pregnant. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. I only kept my trust beneficiary rights if I produced a male heir.When I went into labor, his mother insisted on a "natural" birth for "optimal neurological development." She paid off the high-end private doctor to refuse me an epidural.I nearly bled out on that table. I gave birth to Leo through hell and back.When I woke up, the first thing I heard was his mother's icy voice. "Stubborn as a weed... what a survivor."After that, I was diagnosed with severe postpartum depression.They used my need for psychiatric care as grounds for a protective order and took Leo back to the Boston family estate.I had to put on a cheerful mask for the therapist every single day just to earn a supervised visit on weekends.When I finally recovered and Leo came back to me, he had learned to despise me from his grandparents.He never called me Mom. It was always "that crazy woman" or "the loser." To outsiders, he said I was just an overpaid nanny.I was desperate to fix our relationship. I ignored the psychological abuse and twisted myself into knots trying to please him.But he only had eyes for Victoria.Before the birthday party, I overheard him begging Richard for a brand-new, limited-edition, professional-grade drone.I cashed in every favor I had left to get that drone. I planned to surprise him when he blew out the candles.But when the host asked him his wish, he shot me a look of pure malice and shouted into the microphone: "I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bullet tearing through my chest.The snickers and pitying glances from the society guests were unbearable. And Richard just stood there, letting it happen.I swallowed the humiliation and pain. I kept smiling, kept playing the gracious hostess, greeting guests as they came and went.And in the end, I couldn't even get a proper family photo.This time, I was just done.I wanted out of this suffocating nightmare."Richard, you're not walking out that door until your lawyer sends the email."I grabbed his shirt cuff, refusing to let go."Clara! Are you trying to drive this entire family into a mental institution? Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

headline: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with a
body_text: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with anticipation. "I want Victoria to be my mommy."Everyone burst into laughter, their eyes on me dripping with mockery.Victoria is my husband's chief of staff. He's the CEO of a private equity group. This wasn't the first time my son had said he wanted her as his mother.I didn't want to make a scene at the Hamptons estate, so I let it slide.But when the family portrait from the party arrived at our Upper East Side penthouse, I saw it. My face had been expertly edited and replaced with Victoria's.I asked the photographer. He told me my son had demanded the change.And Richard had approved it.In that moment, I felt nothing but sheer exhaustion....That night, I told Richard I wanted a separation. He didn't even look up from his Bloomberg Terminal."Over a photo? Really?""Yes.""How many times is this now, Clara? Can you stop treating divorce like some kind of game?"Richard pinched the bridge of his nose, looking utterly weary."This time it's real, Richard. Let's contact the lawyers."I stood in front of his massive desk, my voice so steady it sounded foreign to me.Richard just scoffed dismissively and gathered up the M&A documents on the desk. "I don't have time for your theatrics. I have a conference call with the Tokyo executives at dawn."Victoria was waiting by the study door, his bespoke suit jacket draped over her arm. Her posture was rigidly perfect. "Clara, this meeting was initiated directly by several major Wall Street shareholders. It would reflect poorly on the stock price if Richard is late."She looked at me with a hint of reproach, as if I were a child throwing a tantrum.A surge of anger ignited in my chest. I snapped, "Who gave you permission to speak in this house?""Clara!" Richard slammed his hand on the desk, rattling his whiskey glass.Victoria's eyes reddened. Her voice tightened. "Richard, I'm fine. Please don't fight with her because of me."Just then, her phone rang.When she answered, she "accidentally" hit the speakerphone button. A smug male voice blasted from the receiver."Victoria, where's your star CEO? Not on the road yet?""Don't tell me that hysterical control freak has him locked down at home again?""Honestly, Richard should just sign the papers with that neurotic wife of his and marry you. Look at you—Harvard grad, young, gorgeous, sharp as a tack. You and Richard are the real 'power couple'."I recognized the voice. It was Carter, Richard's business partner.He used to lay it on thick, always calling me the perfect hostess of high society."Carter, don't be ridiculous. We'll be down in a minute."Victoria fumbled to hang up, but a faint blush crept up her ears."I'm sorry, Clara. Carter was just making one of those crass Silicon Valley jokes."I ignored her. I stared hard at Richard. "So this is how your partner talks about me behind my back? A control freak? A hysterical lunatic?"Richard rubbed his temples in frustration. "Clara, why do you always have to play the victim and nitpick every little thing?""Whatever people say is protected by the First Amendment. I can't police that."He grabbed his briefcase and moved to walk past me.As he brushed by, the scent of his cologne—identical to Victoria's—snapped the last thread of my sanity.I threw myself in front of him like a woman possessed. Is this really the hill you want to die on?""I already told PR to track down the original files and reprint them for next month's country club newsletter. Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

headline: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with a
body_text: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with anticipation. "I want Victoria to be my mommy."Everyone burst into laughter, their eyes on me dripping with mockery.Victoria is my husband's chief of staff. He's the CEO of a private equity group. This wasn't the first time my son had said he wanted her as his mother.I didn't want to make a scene at the Hamptons estate, so I let it slide.But when the family portrait from the party arrived at our Upper East Side penthouse, I saw it. My face had been expertly edited and replaced with Victoria's.I asked the photographer. He told me my son had demanded the change.And Richard had approved it.In that moment, I felt nothing but sheer exhaustion....That night, I told Richard I wanted a separation. He didn't even look up from his Bloomberg Terminal."Over a photo? Really?""Yes.""How many times is this now, Clara? Can you stop treating divorce like some kind of game?"Richard pinched the bridge of his nose, looking utterly weary."This time it's real, Richard. Let's contact the lawyers."I stood in front of his massive desk, my voice so steady it sounded foreign to me.Richard just scoffed dismissively and gathered up the M&A documents on the desk. "I don't have time for your theatrics. I have a conference call with the Tokyo executives at dawn."Victoria was waiting by the study door, his bespoke suit jacket draped over her arm. Her posture was rigidly perfect. "Clara, this meeting was initiated directly by several major Wall Street shareholders. It would reflect poorly on the stock price if Richard is late."She looked at me with a hint of reproach, as if I were a child throwing a tantrum.A surge of anger ignited in my chest. I snapped, "Who gave you permission to speak in this house?""Clara!" Richard slammed his hand on the desk, rattling his whiskey glass.Victoria's eyes reddened. Her voice tightened. "Richard, I'm fine. Please don't fight with her because of me."Just then, her phone rang.When she answered, she "accidentally" hit the speakerphone button. A smug male voice blasted from the receiver."Victoria, where's your star CEO? Not on the road yet?""Don't tell me that hysterical control freak has him locked down at home again?""Honestly, Richard should just sign the papers with that neurotic wife of his and marry you. Look at you—Harvard grad, young, gorgeous, sharp as a tack. You and Richard are the real 'power couple'."I recognized the voice. It was Carter, Richard's business partner.He used to lay it on thick, always calling me the perfect hostess of high society."Carter, don't be ridiculous. We'll be down in a minute."Victoria fumbled to hang up, but a faint blush crept up her ears."I'm sorry, Clara. Carter was just making one of those crass Silicon Valley jokes."I ignored her. I stared hard at Richard. "So this is how your partner talks about me behind my back? A control freak? A hysterical lunatic?"Richard rubbed his temples in frustration. "Clara, why do you always have to play the victim and nitpick every little thing?""Whatever people say is protected by the First Amendment. I can't police that."He grabbed his briefcase and moved to walk past me.As he brushed by, the scent of his cologne—identical to Victoria's—snapped the last thread of my sanity.I threw myself in front of him like a woman possessed. My voice was shrill, unrecognizable. "Call your lawyer! Right now!"Richard's face darkened. His jaw was clenched tight, and the veins on his hand gripping the briefcase bulged. "It's just a photo! Is this really the hill you want to die on?""I already told PR to track down the original files and reprint them for next month's country club newsletter. Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. He didn't even get a plea deal before the FBI hauled him away.His trust assets were frozen. Every ally in politics and business vanished overnight.Richard's parents were terrified the scandal would taint their precious family name.The day after my dad was sentenced, they summoned me to their estate in Connecticut and pressured me to sign a postnuptial agreement and file for divorce.Then I got pregnant. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. I only kept my trust beneficiary rights if I produced a male heir.When I went into labor, his mother insisted on a "natural" birth for "optimal neurological development." She paid off the high-end private doctor to refuse me an epidural.I nearly bled out on that table. I gave birth to Leo through hell and back.When I woke up, the first thing I heard was his mother's icy voice. "Stubborn as a weed... what a survivor."After that, I was diagnosed with severe postpartum depression.They used my need for psychiatric care as grounds for a protective order and took Leo back to the Boston family estate.I had to put on a cheerful mask for the therapist every single day just to earn a supervised visit on weekends.When I finally recovered and Leo came back to me, he had learned to despise me from his grandparents.He never called me Mom. It was always "that crazy woman" or "the loser." To outsiders, he said I was just an overpaid nanny.I was desperate to fix our relationship. I ignored the psychological abuse and twisted myself into knots trying to please him.But he only had eyes for Victoria.Before the birthday party, I overheard him begging Richard for a brand-new, limited-edition, professional-grade drone.I cashed in every favor I had left to get that drone. I planned to surprise him when he blew out the candles.But when the host asked him his wish, he shot me a look of pure malice and shouted into the microphone: "I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bullet tearing through my chest.The snickers and pitying glances from the society guests were unbearable. And Richard just stood there, letting it happen.I swallowed the humiliation and pain. I kept smiling, kept playing the gracious hostess, greeting guests as they came and went.And in the end, I couldn't even get a proper family photo.This time, I was just done.I wanted out of this suffocating nightmare."Richard, you're not walking out that door until your lawyer sends the email."I grabbed his shirt cuff, refusing to let go."Clara! Are you trying to drive this entire family into a mental institution? Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. It would reflect poorly on the stock price if Richard is late."She looked at me with a hint of reproach, as if I were a child throwing a tantrum.A surge of anger ignited in my chest. I snapped, "Who gave you permission to speak in this house?""Clara!" Richard slammed his hand on the desk, rattling his whiskey glass.Victoria's eyes reddened. Her voice tightened. "Richard, I'm fine. Please don't fight with her because of me."Just then, her phone rang.When she answered, she "accidentally" hit the speakerphone button. A smug male voice blasted from the receiver."Victoria, where's your star CEO? Not on the road yet?""Don't tell me that hysterical control freak has him locked down at home again?""Honestly, Richard should just sign the papers with that neurotic wife of his and marry you. Look at you—Harvard grad, young, gorgeous, sharp as a tack. You and Richard are the real 'power couple'."I recognized the voice. It was Carter, Richard's business partner.He used to lay it on thick, always calling me the perfect hostess of high society."Carter, don't be ridiculous. We'll be down in a minute."Victoria fumbled to hang up, but a faint blush crept up her ears."I'm sorry, Clara. Carter was just making one of those crass Silicon Valley jokes."I ignored her. I stared hard at Richard. "So this is how your partner talks about me behind my back? A control freak? A hysterical lunatic?"Richard rubbed his temples in frustration. "Clara, why do you always have to play the victim and nitpick every little thing?""Whatever people say is protected by the First Amendment. I can't police that."He grabbed his briefcase and moved to walk past me.As he brushed by, the scent of his cologne—identical to Victoria's—snapped the last thread of my sanity.I threw myself in front of him like a woman possessed. My voice was shrill, unrecognizable. "Call your lawyer! Right now!"Richard's face darkened. His jaw was clenched tight, and the veins on his hand gripping the briefcase bulged. "It's just a photo! Is this really the hill you want to die on?""I already told PR to track down the original files and reprint them for next month's country club newsletter. Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. He didn't even get a plea deal before the FBI hauled him away.His trust assets were frozen. Every ally in politics and business vanished overnight.Richard's parents were terrified the scandal would taint their precious family name.The day after my dad was sentenced, they summoned me to their estate in Connecticut and pressured me to sign a postnuptial agreement and file for divorce.Then I got pregnant. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. I only kept my trust beneficiary rights if I produced a male heir.When I went into labor, his mother insisted on a "natural" birth for "optimal neurological development." She paid off the high-end private doctor to refuse me an epidural.I nearly bled out on that table. I gave birth to Leo through hell and back.When I woke up, the first thing I heard was his mother's icy voice. "Stubborn as a weed... what a survivor."After that, I was diagnosed with severe postpartum depression.They used my need for psychiatric care as grounds for a protective order and took Leo back to the Boston family estate.I had to put on a cheerful mask for the therapist every single day just to earn a supervised visit on weekends.When I finally recovered and Leo came back to me, he had learned to despise me from his grandparents.He never called me Mom. It was always "that crazy woman" or "the loser." To outsiders, he said I was just an overpaid nanny.I was desperate to fix our relationship. I ignored the psychological abuse and twisted myself into knots trying to please him.But he only had eyes for Victoria.Before the birthday party, I overheard him begging Richard for a brand-new, limited-edition, professional-grade drone.I cashed in every favor I had left to get that drone. I planned to surprise him when he blew out the candles.But when the host asked him his wish, he shot me a look of pure malice and shouted into the microphone: "I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bullet tearing through my chest.The snickers and pitying glances from the society guests were unbearable. And Richard just stood there, letting it happen.I swallowed the humiliation and pain. I kept smiling, kept playing the gracious hostess, greeting guests as they came and went.And in the end, I couldn't even get a proper family photo.This time, I was just done.I wanted out of this suffocating nightmare."Richard, you're not walking out that door until your lawyer sends the email."I grabbed his shirt cuff, refusing to let go."Clara! Are you trying to drive this entire family into a mental institution? Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever? Then Leo could live in this building with you always!"Victoria: "Leo, maybe save those feelings for Dr. Reynolds. If she sees this, she'll totally lose it again..."My vision blurred. Every ounce of warmth drained from my body.My husband, my son, and another woman had formed their own "Perfect Family."It was beyond absurd. It was more grotesque than any off-Broadway farce.The watch fell to the hardwood floor. The screen splintered into pieces."Ah! You evil witch! You loser! I'll get Daddy's lawyer to sue you! You owe me a new watch!"Leo was red in the face, screaming every curse word he'd learned online and at his private academy.Not satisfied, he snatched up the broken watch and hurled it at my head with all his might.A sharp pain exploded, followed by a warm trickle.I touched my forehead. My fingers came away stained with bright red."Leo! That's enough!" Richard finally intervened, grabbing Leo's arm. He pulled a silk handkerchief from his pocket and offered it to me."Sometimes, when a child feels distant from you, you need to discuss the reasons with your own therapist.""Being this aggressive and lacking any emotional control—who would want to be around you?""Alright. You got your apology. Satisfied now?"The blood soaked through the silk instantly. I didn't reply.I looked at the three people standing across from me.My husband, positioned protectively in front of Victoria.My son, wedged between the two of them, glaring at me with unbridled rage.And Victoria, clutching Leo with a look of deep maternal concern, as if she were the legal guardian.What a picture-perfect, all-American family."Tell your attorney to draft the agreement." My voice was eerily calm."I'm done. I want out of this marriage."

headline: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with a
body_text: At my son's birthday party, the host asked him what his biggest wish was.My son's face lit up with anticipation. "I want Victoria to be my mommy."Everyone burst into laughter, their eyes on me dripping with mockery.Victoria is my husband's chief of staff. He's the CEO of a private equity group. This wasn't the first time my son had said he wanted her as his mother.I didn't want to make a scene at the Hamptons estate, so I let it slide.But when the family portrait from the party arrived at our Upper East Side penthouse, I saw it. My face had been expertly edited and replaced with Victoria's.I asked the photographer. He told me my son had demanded the change.And Richard had approved it.In that moment, I felt nothing but sheer exhaustion....That night, I told Richard I wanted a separation. He didn't even look up from his Bloomberg Terminal."Over a photo? Really?""Yes.""How many times is this now, Clara? Can you stop treating divorce like some kind of game?"Richard pinched the bridge of his nose, looking utterly weary."This time it's real, Richard. Let's contact the lawyers."I stood in front of his massive desk, my voice so steady it sounded foreign to me.Richard just scoffed dismissively and gathered up the M&A documents on the desk. "I don't have time for your theatrics. I have a conference call with the Tokyo executives at dawn."Victoria was waiting by the study door, his bespoke suit jacket draped over her arm. Her posture was rigidly perfect. "Clara, this meeting was initiated directly by several major Wall Street shareholders. It would reflect poorly on the stock price if Richard is late."She looked at me with a hint of reproach, as if I were a child throwing a tantrum.A surge of anger ignited in my chest. I snapped, "Who gave you permission to speak in this house?""Clara!" Richard slammed his hand on the desk, rattling his whiskey glass.Victoria's eyes reddened. Her voice tightened. "Richard, I'm fine. Please don't fight with her because of me."Just then, her phone rang.When she answered, she "accidentally" hit the speakerphone button. A smug male voice blasted from the receiver."Victoria, where's your star CEO? Not on the road yet?""Don't tell me that hysterical control freak has him locked down at home again?""Honestly, Richard should just sign the papers with that neurotic wife of his and marry you. Look at you—Harvard grad, young, gorgeous, sharp as a tack. You and Richard are the real 'power couple'."I recognized the voice. It was Carter, Richard's business partner.He used to lay it on thick, always calling me the perfect hostess of high society."Carter, don't be ridiculous. We'll be down in a minute."Victoria fumbled to hang up, but a faint blush crept up her ears."I'm sorry, Clara. Carter was just making one of those crass Silicon Valley jokes."I ignored her. I stared hard at Richard. "So this is how your partner talks about me behind my back? A control freak? A hysterical lunatic?"Richard rubbed his temples in frustration. "Clara, why do you always have to play the victim and nitpick every little thing?""Whatever people say is protected by the First Amendment. I can't police that."He grabbed his briefcase and moved to walk past me.As he brushed by, the scent of his cologne—identical to Victoria's—snapped the last thread of my sanity.I threw myself in front of him like a woman possessed. My voice was shrill, unrecognizable. "Call your lawyer! Right now!"Richard's face darkened. His jaw was clenched tight, and the veins on his hand gripping the briefcase bulged. "It's just a photo! Is this really the hill you want to die on?""I already told PR to track down the original files and reprint them for next month's country club newsletter. Happy?""Leo asked for Victoria in the photo because you spend all your time at those useless charity galas. Victoria not only helps me run the business, but she also makes time to take him to the equestrian club! What's so wrong about a kid wanting a picture with someone he actually likes?""Maybe you should ask yourself what kind of mother you've been lately."Victoria's eyes welled up with tears. She seized the moment to speak softly. "I'm so sorry, Clara. Maybe I've been spending too much time with Leo on weekends in Central Park. But Richard has been working around the clock on this Silicon Valley tech acquisition for over a week. I'm begging you, please don't add to his stress right now."I looked at the two of them.One was playing the martyr. The other was seething with suppressed rage.I had become the villain in the room—the unhinged harpy.But they were the ones who had pushed me to this edge, step by calculated step.}}}###Chapter 2###{{{My marriage to Richard was a classic merger of new Manhattan money and old Boston bloodlines.Two years in, my father was hit with insider trading charges by the SEC. He didn't even get a plea deal before the FBI hauled him away.His trust assets were frozen. Every ally in politics and business vanished overnight.Richard's parents were terrified the scandal would taint their precious family name.The day after my dad was sentenced, they summoned me to their estate in Connecticut and pressured me to sign a postnuptial agreement and file for divorce.Then I got pregnant. They made me sign a brutal custody memorandum. I only kept my trust beneficiary rights if I produced a male heir.When I went into labor, his mother insisted on a "natural" birth for "optimal neurological development." She paid off the high-end private doctor to refuse me an epidural.I nearly bled out on that table. I gave birth to Leo through hell and back.When I woke up, the first thing I heard was his mother's icy voice. "Stubborn as a weed... what a survivor."After that, I was diagnosed with severe postpartum depression.They used my need for psychiatric care as grounds for a protective order and took Leo back to the Boston family estate.I had to put on a cheerful mask for the therapist every single day just to earn a supervised visit on weekends.When I finally recovered and Leo came back to me, he had learned to despise me from his grandparents.He never called me Mom. It was always "that crazy woman" or "the loser." To outsiders, he said I was just an overpaid nanny.I was desperate to fix our relationship. I ignored the psychological abuse and twisted myself into knots trying to please him.But he only had eyes for Victoria.Before the birthday party, I overheard him begging Richard for a brand-new, limited-edition, professional-grade drone.I cashed in every favor I had left to get that drone. I planned to surprise him when he blew out the candles.But when the host asked him his wish, he shot me a look of pure malice and shouted into the microphone: "I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bullet tearing through my chest.The snickers and pitying glances from the society guests were unbearable. And Richard just stood there, letting it happen.I swallowed the humiliation and pain. I kept smiling, kept playing the gracious hostess, greeting guests as they came and went.And in the end, I couldn't even get a proper family photo.This time, I was just done.I wanted out of this suffocating nightmare."Richard, you're not walking out that door until your lawyer sends the email."I grabbed his shirt cuff, refusing to let go."Clara! Are you trying to drive this entire family into a mental institution? Is that what it takes?"He seized the crystal whiskey glass from the desk and hurled it onto the rug."Ah!"Victoria screamed and stumbled back on her heels.Richard immediately stepped in front of her, shielding her body with his own. He patted her shoulder, murmuring reassurances.He didn't even notice the shards of glass slicing into my bare foot. Blood started to seep across the floor."Richard." Victoria's voice trembled with fake tears. "I should have just skipped tonight's meeting and missed the party... I never imagined I'd cause such a fight between you two.""It's not your fault." Richard's sharp features softened with regret. "This is my failure to manage internal family matters."Victoria sniffled and tugged gently at his sleeve. "Right now, the priority is helping Clara calm down. We need to be rational."One sentence. That was all it took for the raging Wall Street titan to regain his composure.He hit the intercom and told the housekeeper to bring Leo down.A moment later, Leo shuffled downstairs in his silk pajamas.The second he saw Victoria, he tore away from the housekeeper and launched himself into her arms. "Victoria, I missed you so much."Victoria scooped him up and kissed his blond hair. "I missed you too, sweetie."Leo noticed the smudged mascara on her perfect face. He scrunched up his little brow and shook his fist."Why are you crying? Who was mean to you? I'll sue them!"From start to finish, Leo didn't glance at me once.My voice came out rough, like a rusty blade scraping stone. "Leo, come to Mommy."Leo glared at me with pure hatred. "You're not my mommy! I don't want a criminal's daughter for a mommy!"Those words were a dull knife, sawing slowly and brutally through my heart.I thought I might double over from the pain.}}}###Chapter 3###{{{"Leo! Watch your language!" Richard scolded, but there was no real weight behind it."Richard, he's just a child. He needs positive guidance," Victoria interjected instantly.Leo clung to her tighter, as if she were his shield. "Victoria is so nice. Not like some people... always creating a toxic environment with Daddy!""Leo!" Richard finally looked stern. But I knew it wasn't for my sake. It was the elite breeding in him, embarrassed by the ugly display."Apologize to your mother. If you don't, she's going to move out."Leo's blue eyes lit up with undisguised glee. "The crazy lady is finally leaving our apartment?"He turned to me, his face a mask of triumphant joy. "I won't apologize! Just leave already! I want Victoria to be my mommy!"It felt like a bayonet twisting in my gut. Again and again.I started to wonder if this was really the child I'd nearly died bringing into the world. The child who shared half my blood.Richard's brow furrowed into a knot. "I said stop that attitude! Apologize. Now. It's about principle."Leo had inherited his father's arrogance and stubbornness.Red-faced, he shook his head, jaw clenched."No! I hate her!"Even Richard seemed at a loss. Years of absentee parenting had left him permissive with the boy.It was Victoria who crouched down and used that child-psychology voice. "Sweetie, just say you're sorry. For me. Okay?"Leo chewed his lip, hesitated, and then nodded."Sorry. There. Happy?"Richard exhaled, his shoulders relaxing."He apologized. Now stop with the hysterics.""Quit while you're ahead, Clara."Just then, Victoria's smartwatch buzzed twice.She glanced at the screen and swiped quickly.Then, the kid's watch on Leo's wrist buzzed twice as well.For the next two minutes, their devices chimed back and forth like a frantic stock ticker.A strange, knowing smirk played on Leo's lips. He snuck a glance at me.My stomach dropped. A cold dread washed over me.I realized they were having a private iMessage conversation.And I was the topic.My hands started to shake uncontrollably.Before I knew what I was doing, I lunged forward and ripped the watch off Leo's wrist."You psycho! That's my private property!" Leo shrieked and flew at me.His small fists rained down on my face and neck.I ignored the pain and stared at that tiny screen.Leo, Richard, and Victoria had a group chat. It was titled "Perfect Family."Leo: "That crazy woman is so disgusting! Leo hates her!"Victoria: "Leo, you shouldn't say things like that~ But I do feel just awful seeing you so upset."Leo: "Why doesn't she just go away forever?
